author: cursor[bot]
body:
### Inconsistent String coercion risks TypeError on numeric fields

**Medium Severity**

<!-- DESCRIPTION START -->
`position.entryPrice`, `position.takeProfitPrice`, `position.stopLossPrice`, and `position.liquidationPrice` have `.replace()` called directly on them, which will throw a `TypeError` if any of these are numbers. Meanwhile, `position.cumulativeFunding.sinceOpen` is defensively wrapped in `String()` before `.replace()`, indicating awareness that some position fields may not be strings. The old code used template literals (`$${value}`), which safely coerce both strings and numbers. The new code is strictly less safe for non-string values.
<!-- DESCRIPTION END -->

<!-- BUGBOT_BUG_ID: ad3e8ded-5f43-45a4-8804-cd81598434e1 -->
action: Reworked the perps formatting change onto the shared controller formatter path and normalized runtime values so numeric fields no longer depend on string-only operations.
